articles.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
articles.snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model enabling efficient on-device function calling for mobile applications. This allows complex tasks to be executed offline, enhancing user experience with low latency.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes mobile AI by enabling offline complex task execution.

articles.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem

articles.ai_summary: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools, allowing developers to create more capable AI agents. This ecosystem supports connections to platforms like GitHub and Notion.
articles.why_it_matters: Expands capabilities for developers to build versatile AI applications.

articles.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
articles.snippet: Google I/O 2026 is returning May 19-20 at Shoreline Amphitheatre in Mountain View, CA. But before the keynotes begin, you can get into the spirit of the event with our annual tradition: the save the date puzzle.  This year's experience highlights how AI can empower and accelerate
articles.ai_summary: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring an engaging save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to energize participants ahead of the keynotes.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights Google's commitment to AI engagement before major events.

articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
articles.snippet: While keynotes are available online, Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas offers an irreplaceable in-person experience centered on networking, hands-on problem solving, and the transition to agentic AI. The event features specialized technical tracks covering everything from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security on Cloud Run, providing developers with the tools to balance individual speed with organizational stability. Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas emphasizes in-person networking and hands-on problem solving, focusing on advancements in agentic AI and specialized technical tracks.
articles.why_it_matters: Promotes valuable in-person experiences for developers in AI and cloud technologies.

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI.
articles.why_it_matters: Strengthens Google's position in high-performance AI deployment.

articles.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
articles.snippet: Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly, interactive "technical gym session" led by Google Cloud experts to help developers and architects sharpen their cloud skills. Moving beyond passive slide decks, the program focuses on hands-on building, covering advanced topics like AI agents, Vertex AI, and developer productivity tools. Each hour-long session is designed to provide tangible results that participants can immediately deploy into their own workflows. It serves as a consistent, dedicated space for
articles.ai_summary: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud experts aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building and practical applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Encourages active learning and immediate application of cloud skills.

articles.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
articles.snippet: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced a suite of updates focused on streamlining the core coding workflow through high-velocity tools like Agent Mode with Auto Approve and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements, along with new features for precise context management and custom commands, aim to transform the AI from a general assistant into a highly tailored, seamless collaborator that adapts to your specific development style.
articles.ai_summary: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced updates to streamline coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views, enhancing collaboration between developers and AI.
articles.why_it_matters: Transforms AI into a more tailored and effective coding collaborator.

articles.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
articles.snippet: In January, folk artist Murphy Campbell discovered several songs on her Spotify profile that did not belong there. They were songs that she had recorded, but she'd never uploaded them to Spotify, and something was off about the vocals. She quickly surmised that someone had pulled performances of the songs she posted to YouTube, created […]

articles.title: Why does Elon require Grok subscriptions for banks? #tech
articles.snippet: What Musk required banks to do for SpaceX’s IPO A report on SpaceX’s impending IPO says Elon Musk is requiring banks that want roles in the offering to take two steps: subscribe to his AI chatbot Grok and advertise it on X. The requirement is significant beca…
articles.ai_summary: Elon Musk is requiring banks involved in SpaceX's IPO to subscribe to his AI chatbot Grok and promote it on X. This move highlights Musk's strategy to integrate his ventures and leverage AI in financial dealings.
articles.why_it_matters: Reveals Musk's unique approach to intertwining technology and finance.

articles.title: Danish Architect Thomas Juul-Hansen on Shaping West Chelsea’s Next Chapter
articles.snippet: The Danish architect has been tapped to design a new condominium at 550 West 21st Street, one of West Chelsea’s last major waterfront development sites.

articles.title: OpenAI Acquires Live Tech Talk Show TBPN
articles.snippet:
OpenAI is acquiring Technology Business Programming Network (TBPN), a daily live tech podcast popular among people working in the Silicon Valley ecosystem.
The post OpenAI Acquires Live Tech Talk Show TBPN appeared first on Thurrott.com.
articles.ai_summary: OpenAI is acquiring the Technology Business Programming Network (TBPN), a live tech podcast popular in Silicon Valley, enhancing its media presence.
articles.why_it_matters: Expands OpenAI's influence in tech media and community engagement.

articles.title: Dell Pro Rugged 12 Tablet
articles.snippet: Rugged tablets won’t win any fashion contests, but they'll survive conditions that would send ordinary electronics to an early grave. Dell’s Pro Rugged 12 Tablet (starts at $3,561; $5,248 as tested) can shrug off four-foot drops, water sprays, dusty environme…
articles.ai_summary: The Dell Pro Rugged 12 Tablet is designed for extreme conditions, capable of withstanding drops and water exposure, catering to demanding environments.
articles.why_it_matters: Addresses the need for durable technology in challenging work settings.

articles.title: Microsoft says Copilot is for entertainment purposes only, not serious use — firm pushing AI hard to consumers tells users not to rely on it for important advice
articles.snippet: Microsoft Copilot's terms of service explicitly say that it's "for entertainment purposes only," but its marketing says otherwise.
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's Copilot is marketed heavily, yet its terms of service state it is only for entertainment, cautioning users against relying on it for serious advice.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises concerns about the reliability of AI tools marketed for serious applications.
